schema.description: A payroll that has been transitioned back to unprocessed state after cancellation.

payroll_deadline.description: A timestamp that is the deadline for the payroll to be run in order for employees to be paid on time.  If payroll has not been run by the deadline, a prepare request will update both the check date and deadline to reflect the soonest employees can be paid and the deadline by which the payroll must be run in order for said check date to be met.
payroll_deadline.title: Payroll-Deadline-Type

check_date.description: The date on which employees will be paid for the payroll.
check_date.title: Payroll-Check-Date-Type

processed.description: Whether or not the payroll has been successfully processed. Note that processed payrolls cannot be updated. Additionally, a payroll is not guaranteed to be processed just because the payroll deadline has passed. Late payrolls are not uncommon. Conversely, users may choose to run payroll before the payroll deadline.
processed.title: Payroll-Processed-Type

processed_date.description: The date at which the payroll was processed. Null if the payroll isn't processed yet.
processed_date.title: Payroll-Processed-Date-Type

calculated_at.description: A timestamp of the last valid payroll calculation. Null if there isn't a valid calculation.
calculated_at.title: Payroll-Calculated-At-Type

company_uuid.description: The UUID of the company for the payroll.
company_uuid.title: Payroll-Company-Uuid-Type

off_cycle.description: Indicates whether the payroll is an off-cycle payroll
off_cycle.title: Payroll-Off-Cycle-Type

off_cycle_reason.description: The off-cycle reason. Only included for off-cycle payrolls.
off_cycle_reason.title: Off-Cycle-Reason-Type

auto_pilot.description: Indicates whether the payroll is an auto pilot payroll
auto_pilot.title: Auto-Pilot-Type

external.description: Indicates whether the payroll is an external payroll
external.title: Payroll-External-Type

start_date.description: The start date, inclusive, of the pay period.

end_date.description: The start date, inclusive, of the pay period.

pay_schedule_uuid.description: The UUID of the pay schedule for the payroll.

created_at.description: Datetime for when the resource was created.
created_at.title: Created-At-Type

partner_owned_disbursement.description: Will money movement for the payroll be performed by the partner rather than by Gusto?
partner_owned_disbursement.title: Payroll-Partner-Owned-Disbursement-Type

schema.title: Unprocessed-Payroll

schema.description:
Not Found 
  
The requested resource does not exist. Make sure the provided ID/UUID is valid.

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

errors.message: The requested resource was not found.

schema.title: Not-Found-Error-Object

422.description:
Unprocessable Entity

This may happen when the body of your request contains errors such as `invalid_attribute_value`, or the request fails due to an `invalid_operation`. See the [Errors Categories](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/error-categories) guide for more details.

schema.description:
Unprocessable Entity
  
This may happen when the body of your request contains errors such as `invalid_attribute_value`, or the request fails due to an `invalid_operation`. See the [Errors Categories](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/error-categories) guide for more details.

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration. If category is `nested_errors`, the object will contain a nested `errors` property with entity errors.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

metadata.description: Contains relevant data to identify the resource in question when applicable. For example, to identify an entity `entity_type` and `entity_uuid` will be provided.

entity_type.description: Name of the entity that the error corresponds to.
Name of the entity that the error corresponds to.

errors.description: Will only exist if category is `nested_errors`. It is possible to have multiple levels of nested errors.
